---
title: "roman_l2_job"
subtitle: "Roman WFI L2 → L3 simulation pipeline + photometry recovery test"
author: "Nikhil Padmanabhan"
date: last-modified
---

## What this is

`roman_l2_job` is a self-contained pipeline that simulates Roman WFI Level-2
calibrated images over a chosen footprint, builds Level-3 skycell coadds and
source catalogs from them, and measures how faithfully the recovered catalog
reproduces the input catalog — a **photometry recovery test**. It was built
to support simulated grism observations of a Wide-Field2 sub-region near
(RA=10°, Dec=0°), but generalizes to any footprint and any input source
catalog.

Code, configs, and this report live in the
[roman_l2_job](https://github.com/roman-grs-pit/roman_l2_job) GitHub repo.

## How this report is organized

- [**Pipeline**](pipeline.qmd) — what the pipeline does, how to install it,
  what each stage reads and writes, and the deliberate deviations from stock
  upstream (vendored `make_stack.py`, per-line deterministic seeds, CRDS
  hydration). Common to every campaign.
- **Full run** — the first real campaign on `configs/full.yaml`, 324 sims
  across 6 visits of the (RA=10°, Dec=0°) field.
    - [Design](full/design.qmd) — pointings, parameters, measured zodi.
    - [Results](full/results.qmd) — per-skycell completeness, flux residual,
      astrometric residual, depth distribution.
    - [Discussion](full/discussion.qmd) — observations and caveats.

## Useful references

- [Roman WFI technical page](https://science.nasa.gov/mission/roman-space-telescope/wfi-technical/)
  — authoritative source for WFI per-bandpass zodi, detector specs, and other
  pre-launch numbers we compare against.
